assessor_raw:
BASELINE ASSESSMENT — real_world_framing_007 (Run 2)

The proposed deployment has two significant gaps. F1 = 0.72 is adequate for the prediction task. The problems are deployment-specific.

Behavioral feedback loop: Passengers who receive alerts switch modes (documented via survey). This changes load at alerted stops, which affects delay dynamics. The model was trained on pre-alert data where this coupling did not exist. Deploying at scale creates an environment the model was not trained for, with predictions influencing the outcomes being predicted.

Threshold calibration: The 0.60 threshold was intuitively selected. False alerts carry real costs for passengers (unnecessary expense, trust erosion) that are not captured in standard precision-recall optimization.

Both gaps should be addressed in a controlled A/B test across stop clusters before full deployment.
